_player_data_note: Player stats pulled from rosters[].roster[].stats — NOT from leaders[] block (leaders only returns 1 top performer per category and misses most players)

key_signals: CORNERS (3, 1, 1 = avg 1.67): Lowest corner average of any knockout-stage team. Structurally low regardless of result or dominance level. Load-bearing for Q10.
SOT CEILING (4, 4, 5): Mexico has NEVER reached 6 SOT in any WC2026 group match — not even in their 3-0 win. Q6 (6+ SOT) is above Mexico's observed ceiling.
CLEAN SHEETS: Three consecutive group-stage clean sheets (RSA, KOR, CZE). Highly organized defensive structure.
RED CARD IN GD1: Played with 10 men in the RSA match and still won 2-0. Indicates defensive resilience even understrength.
GD3 ROTATION: Jimenez rested vs Czechia (not a starter). He returned as starter for this knockout match.

group_matches_pulled.label: OUTLIER — non-competitive opposition, finishing crisis, excluded from competitive-match averaging

averages_competitive_only.note: CUR match excluded as non-competitive outlier

key_signals: BIMODAL FLAG: CUR match (27 shots / 15 SOT / 0 goals) is a structural outlier vs any competitive opposition. Raw averages inflate ECU's apparent attacking output by ~3x.
COMPETITIVE PROFILE: vs CIV (1 SOT, 12 shots) and vs GER (3 SOT, 7 shots) — avg 2.0 SOT and 4.5 corners vs quality opponents.
FINISHING CRISIS RESOLVED: Ecuador scored 0 goals in 2 WC2026 matches despite 16 combined SOT (CIV+CUR). Crisis broke vs GER with 2 goals from only 3 SOT.
CARD-PRONE: Ecuador averaged 3.0 yellow cards in their two competitive matches (CIV: 1, GER: 3). Ecuador drove the 4+ cards YES case.
LOW OFFSIDES: 0 offsides vs CIV, 1 vs GER. Ecuador rarely caught offside even when pressing heavily. This was the KEY SIGNAL for Q12 vs crowd.
